(born August 28, 1958 in [Paterson, New Jersey](/wiki/Paterson,_New_Jersey "Paterson, New Jersey")) is an American [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [promoter](/wiki/Promoter_(entertainment) "Promoter (entertainment)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Abramson, Mitch (20 August 2009). ["American boxing promoter Dino Duva brings China to the ring"](http://www.nydailynews.com/sports/more_sports/2009/08/20/2009-08-20_duva_puts_china_in_ring.html). *New York Daily News*. Retrieved 16 January 2011.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American boxing promoter

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **needs additional [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). [Contentious material](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons#Remove_contentious_material_that_is_unsourced_or_poorly_sourced "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") about living persons that is unsourced or [poorly sourced](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources#Questionable_and_self-published_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources") **must be removed immediately** from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Dino Duva"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Dino+Duva%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Dino+Duva%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Dino+Duva%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Dino+Duva%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Dino+Duva%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Dino+Duva%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2014)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Dino Duva** (born August 28, 1958 in [Paterson, New Jersey](/wiki/Paterson,_New_Jersey "Paterson, New Jersey")) is an American [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") [promoter](/wiki/Promoter_(entertainment) "Promoter (entertainment)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Duva is the son of trainer/manager [Lou Duva](/wiki/Lou_Duva "Lou Duva"), and the younger brother of the late [Dan Duva](/wiki/Dan_Duva "Dan Duva"). The Duvas have been given the title of *"the first family of boxing"*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Dino Duva has promoted boxing events worldwide, and was inducted into the New Jersey Boxing Hall Of Fame in 1999.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He is the former president of his family's '[Main Events](/wiki/Main_Events "Main Events")' boxing promotions. After leaving Main Events in 2000, he founded, with his father, 'Duva Boxing' in [Totowa, New Jersey](/wiki/Totowa,_New_Jersey "Totowa, New Jersey"), which has become a major promoter; he is its current president. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Duva Boxing represents former WBC [Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/Heavyweight_champion "Heavyweight champion") of the World, [Samuel Peter](/wiki/Samuel_Peter "Samuel Peter"), WBO [Cruiserweight](/wiki/Cruiserweight_(boxing) "Cruiserweight (boxing)") Champion [Ola Afolabi](/wiki/Ola_Afolabi "Ola Afolabi"), cruiserweight contender [Darnell Wilson](/wiki/Darnell_Wilson "Darnell Wilson"), [Italian-American](/wiki/Italian-American "Italian-American") heavyweight [Mike Marrone](/wiki/Mike_Marrone "Mike Marrone"), and James De la Rosa.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In December 2006, [Don King](/wiki/Don_King_(boxing_promoter) "Don King (boxing promoter)") purchased a 50% interest in Duva Boxing for an undisclosed financial sum.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] However, Duva still runs Duva Boxing as a separate entity. The merging of "The Duvas" and "The King" drew media and press attention. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
